headline: Week of Mar 9-15: AI models exploding, Anthropic & OpenAI locked in Pentagon drama, agents are here

stories.title: OpenAI drops GPT-5.4 with 1M context window & native computer use
stories.summary: Lowkey the biggest flex this week — GPT-5.4 drops with 1M token context window, native computer use, better token efficiency, and 33% fewer hallucinations than GPT-5.2. Tool Search API redesign hits different for systems with tons of available tools.
stories.takeaway: OpenAI's computer use + massive context window shifts AI from assistant to actual workflow collaborator. Enterprise stuff about to go wild.

stories.title: Anthropic's $100M Partner Network + Pentagon lawsuit escalates military AI divide
stories.summary: Anthropic literally just committed $100M to enterprise partnerships while fighting the Pentagon over guardrails on autonomous weapons & mass surveillance. Revenue jumped to ~$20B annualized, but federal blacklist could tank everything.
stories.takeaway: This fr is the defining tension of 2026: companies building power AI but refusing unrestricted military use. OpenAI capitulated; Anthropic doubled down.

stories.title: Claude Code basically broke software engineering workflows, Anthropic hit $1B ARR in 6 months
stories.summary: Engineers shipping in hours what used to take weeks. GitHub Copilot at 15M paid seats, but Claude Code already doing $2.5B annualized by February. Wall Street literally freaked out — IBM lost $40B in market cap when Anthropic mentioned COBOL translation.
stories.takeaway: Not hype: AI coding genuinely shifted how engineering teams operate. Smaller teams, crazy fast velocity, older systems getting reimagined.

stories.title: Meta planning 20% workforce cuts as AI efficiency gains hit, DeepMind launches 'automated research labs' with robotics
stories.summary: Meta's execs told managers to start planning layoffs (16K employees) to offset $600B AI infrastructure bet. Meanwhile DeepMind + Boston Dynamics dropping Gemini Robotics for humanoid Atlas robots; automated research lab opens in UK 2026 for materials science.
stories.takeaway: Two stories, same truth: AI making labor cheaper. Less people needed per dollar of output. The scaling is actually happening.

watchNext.title: Anthropic vs Pentagon: The supply-chain risk designation could reshape US AI policy
watchNext.why: Next hearing March 24. If Anthropic loses, entire industry safety guardrail approach collapses under federal pressure. If they win, it's precedent for AI ethics over military demands.

watchNext.title: DeepSeek V4 (1T params) hitting production + Chinese AI labs dominating Hugging Face downloads
watchNext.why: Open models from China literally beating closed US APIs now. V4's 40% memory reduction + sparse FP8 changes the inference economics game. Worth tracking how this reshapes global AI deployment.
